journal_entry.description: Employee working meeting meal expense paid by corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Meal expense for a working meeting among employees qualifies as a business meal expense under IFRS. The transaction clearly states the business purpose (working meeting among employees), so this is deductible business entertainment. The corporate credit card creates a liability (credit card payable). No HST/GST is recoverable on meals as only 50% of meal expenses are deductible for tax purposes in Canada, but the full amount is recorded as an expense with the tax restriction applied at year-end tax filing. The debit tuple (0,0,1,0,0,0) correctly identifies this as an expense increase, and credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly identifies a liability increase.

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable increases by $125)

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (credit card payable increases by $125)

output.reason: Meal expense for a working meeting = expense increase (1 line). The debit increases the meals & entertainment expense account.

debit_classifier.reason: Meal expense for a working meeting = expense increase (1 line). The debit increases the meals & entertainment expense account.
